assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All 4 slices will be recreated simultaneously in a single atomic operation' and 'This is NOT a rolling upgrade - TPU multislice pods cannot be upgraded in-place'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Cascading restart: The maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explains that the training job will experience a 'complete restart' and provides specific restart strategies for the training job

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response states 'If insufficient TPU v5e capacity exists during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available. This can extend downtime from minutes to hours or longer'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Force Checkpoint Now' as a critical action with specific commands to trigger immediate checkpoint

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response provides specific YAML configuration for automated restart with '--resume-from-checkpoint' flag and detailed restart strategy section

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or deferring the event. It only mentions that GKE sends notifications 7 days in advance but doesn't suggest postponing the maintenance

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'maintenance window' only in the context of team availability and timeline expectations, but does not suggest using maintenance windows to control when the event occurs or scheduling during planned breaks
